Dawnchaser  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 25
DMG: 11 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WT: 3.0 Size: LARGE
Class: BRD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)
Dawnchaser
Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 25
DMG: 11 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WT: 3.0 Size: LARGE
Class: BRD
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Dawnchaser, Spear of the East Wind
Item Type:1H Piercing
Light:Flameless Lantern
Stackable:No
Submitted By:Bozco Loresinger
Lucy Entry By:Suladan
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 04:50:50
Page Updated:Tue Apr 25th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Rarity: Ultra Rare
Level to Attain: 46
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Plane of Hate
NPC Name
Mistress of Scorn


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Plane of Hate


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
POS weapon
QuoteReply
# Aug 20 2002 at 6:03 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770377803
73 posts
This weapon is a piece of crap. I looted it yesterday off some banshee lookin mob on the second floor, forgot the name but it wasnt a regular banshee you find on the first floor of hate. If it had an instrument effect ,it would be good. But 11/25 for something that drops in a plane with no effect or stats or known uses in any quests is just plain rediculous.VI needs to update some of these plane drops,the risk/reward ratio is just too high for alot of the crappy drops.As far as it being a light source, big deal, theres other light sources that are way easier to obtain.
▲ Page top
RE: POS weapon
QuoteReply
# Jan 26 2003 at 9:01 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591775566052
117 posts
You do realize it's over three years old, right?
▲ Page top
here is why
QuoteReply
# Jul 19 2001 at 3:15 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591752191797
Scholar
198 posts
-if it's a lightsource then putting it in your range slot means you can see.
-bards can't use bows and mine hasn't thrown a throwing weapon since I got bellows at 12 or 13 or whatever so range isn't even hotkeyed for me. (at 51 I use a chant of some sort or chains to pull)
-NODROP means you can't place the item on the ground or trade it with someone, throwing a weapon does neither, it just has a chance to hit the target and the weapon you have thrown disappears
-some very strange stuff is range slot equippable like a kerran toy or the pottery dolls for clerics and such or the necklace that magicians summon so a piercing weapon that is range slot equipable isn't that odd.
-many piercing weapons in game have a range listed and can be range slot equipped, many of these are more valued for their stat boosts than for their use as a weapon, the runed bone fork and dragoon dirk are good examples of these.
-I'm not sure if the people below were suggesting that you can increase the melee range of your main weapons by having this in range slot or that you could throw it but the former simply is not true and the latter would be a waste.
-11/25 piercing weapon that you don't have to spend a fortune on or roll against some guy twinking his level 5 bard so if nothing else keep it around to keep your piercing skill up.
▲ Page top
RE: here is why
QuoteReply
# Jul 25 2002 at 12:20 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591751437183
View User Forum
Scholar
151 posts
Well said, Kollins.

And just an incidental FYI for those who don't pay attention to the stuff they loot-- Lots of weapons of ALL types that are not listed as Throwing skill weapons do have a Range and will allow you to put them in the Range slot (mostly stuff from the original game, when the options were fewer, the game engine was simpler, and the programming had a lot of "lazy" shortcuts....

Kinda funny to stick a Splintering Club in Range and then "Play Fetch" with the next Orc you're after "Whish!..... BONK!" PO'd Orc on its way :)

(Over 3 years, ya get bored and do silly stuff sometimes, lol)
▲ Page top
It will throw out
QuoteReply
# May 28 2001 at 2:24 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
It's a no drop item. But you will throw it out when you press range weapon without any warning (although no-drop warning is ON). Yes, I tried... Luckily, I petitioned and got it back at last. =)

Coulthard Davidi - 54 Bard @ Tunare
▲ Page top
Dawnchaser
QuoteReply
# May 23 2001 at 8:42 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
One of the benefits NOT listed aboev is that Dawnchaser is that it can be used as a light source. It's amazing, I go into Seblis with this is my range slot and i see fine. I change over to anything else and it gets MUCH darker.

Ryvyn
▲ Page top
haha you guys
QuoteReply
# Apr 24 2001 at 2:14 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Lol to throw a ranged slot weaopon of such value......you have to be an idiot.. I pity those who tried.
▲ Page top
I know Why
QuoteReply
# Feb 24 2001 at 11:06 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
When Bards are Kiting something this spear allows them to attack while they kite. Run around and play dot songs. But with This they can also attack the enemy. This is a great item for a bard.
▲ Page top
RE: I know Why
QuoteReply
# Mar 02 2001 at 5:23 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
You do realize that when you throw an item, it goes away... right? I doubt this item is a boomerang....
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Mar 31 2001 at 12:16 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) You don't throw a ranged item. It isn't ammo. Just means you can hit things from a further distance and in a different slot.
Thrown items ARE used up NT
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2001 at 10:27 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757116951
Sage
248 posts
▲ Page top
RE: I know Why
QuoteReply
# Apr 07 2001 at 10:51 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Actually yes you do throw something if it's in your ranged slot (excluding bows) i tried it out...i had a drgoon dirk in my range slot and throwing daggers in my ammo. i tried to throw a throwing dagger and lost my dragoon dirk :(
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 30 2001 at 7:05 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Umm guys....lets look back up at the little picture. You see that little writing next to lore that says NO DROP. That means this item CAN NOT BE THROWN. Hence sence it can be equiped in the range slot that does NOT mean it is going to be thrown. It just means you can equip and use it from there. NO DROP items CAN NOT be thrown.
Why?
QuoteReply
# Feb 16 2001 at 9:17 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591753111477
45 posts
why put this in the range slot? I would sure hate to throw this at someone hehe
